verses.text: The people give thanks, for God's wondrous works are near; in His set time He will judge uprightly and uphold the tottering earth. Judgment belongs to His timing. The God who steadies the world will set all things right at the appointed hour.

The people give thanks, for God's wondrous works are near; in His set time He will judge uprightly and uphold the tottering earth. Judgment belongs to His timing. The God who steadies the world will set all things right at the appointed hour.

The people give thanks, for God's wondrous works are near; in His set time He will judge uprightly and uphold the tottering earth. Judgment belongs to His timing. The God who steadies the world will set all things right at the appointed hour.
He warns the proud, 'Lift not up the horn,' nor speak with a stiff neck. Arrogance is rebuked. To exalt oneself against God is folly that invites His judgment.
He warns the proud, 'Lift not up the horn,' nor speak with a stiff neck. Arrogance is rebuked. To exalt oneself against God is folly that invites His judgment.

Promotion comes not from any direction of earth, 'but God is the judge,' holding a cup the wicked must drink to the dregs. He alone raises and abases. Every rise and fall of men is in the hand of God.

Promotion comes not from any direction of earth, 'but God is the judge,' holding a cup the wicked must drink to the dregs. He alone raises and abases. Every rise and fall of men is in the hand of God.

Promotion comes not from any direction of earth, 'but God is the judge,' holding a cup the wicked must drink to the dregs. He alone raises and abases. Every rise and fall of men is in the hand of God.
Asaph will declare and sing for ever, while God cuts off the horns of the wicked and exalts the righteous. Two destinies are sealed. God brings the proud low and lifts up those who trust Him.
